history.titleZh: 华擎幻影电竞与钢铁传奇360 LCD评测：令人印象深刻的散热首秀
history.descriptionZh:
PC爱好者熟知的华擎（ASRock）以显卡、主板、电源和游戏显示器闻名，现在正式进军液冷散热领域。本次评测的两款产品是Phantom Gaming 360 LCD和Steel Legend 360 LCD，均配备3.4英寸480×480分辨率的IPS屏幕，支持60Hz刷新率和250尼特亮度，可用于显示动画和性能监控数据。两款产品的主要区别在于外观、风扇规格和散热器厚度：Steel Legend采用标准27mm厚度的360mm冷排，而Phantom Gaming使用更厚的32mm冷排，搭配不同的风扇设计。

核心技术层面，两款水冷的CPU冷头均采用罕见的厚铜底设计，类似为AMD Threadripper或Intel Xeon服务器CPU设计的一体式水冷，旨在增强热传导效率。冷头顶部集成一个70mm、3000 RPM的VRM风扇，专门为内存和主板VRM模块进行主动散热。冷头设计确保不会遮挡内存插槽，兼容任何高度的内存条。屏幕控制需使用华擎Polychrome Display软件，支持6种预设主题或自定义，但文件兼容性限制严格：自定义背景图片或视频文件需小于20MB且分辨率不超过1080p。

性能参数方面，两款水冷均支持AMD AM5/AM4和Intel LGA1700/1851平台。Steel Legend 360 LCD配备三把独立的120mm风扇，转速0-2500±10% RPM，最大风量76.7 CFM，风压4.16 mmH₂O；Phantom Gaming 360 LCD则采用一体式风扇模块，转速0-2400±10% RPM，风量61.28 CFM，风压3.11 mmH₂O，但依靠更厚的32mm冷排平衡散热性能。在测试中，两者均能压制AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D超过260W的TDP。噪音表现上，Phantom Gaming风扇功率较低（对应更安静），而Steel Legend风扇更强劲但噪音更大。保修方面，Phantom Gaming提供6年保修，Steel Legend仅2年，价格分别为189.99美元和159.99美元。

测试环境方面，为了反映真实使用场景，作者采用封闭机箱（Tryx Flova F50）进行测试，而非开放式测试台，以避免弱化散热需求导致虚高成绩。CPU使用Ryzen 9 9950X3D，其CCD热分布更均衡，相比两侧温差大的9950X更具代表性。显卡为MSI RTX 4070 Ti Super，内存TeamGroup DDR5-7200，主板MSI X870E Carbon Wifi。安装过程详细描述：先安装360冷排至机箱，拆除原装AM4/5扣具，放置固定支架并锁紧螺丝，涂抹散热膏后安装冷头（建议水管朝南以获得最佳性能），最后滑入LCD屏幕并连接USB、PWM和ARGB线缆。

这两款水冷直接对标市场上高端一体式水冷，通过差异化定位满足不同用户：Steel Legend以简洁白色设计、更低价格和标准性能吸引实用派；Phantom Gaming以更厚冷排、ARGB灯效（含风扇叶片和冷排侧边灯带）、更长保修和更强静音表现吸引追求性能与灯效的玩家。华擎借此补齐了散热产品线，与主板、显卡形成生态协同，其厚铜底和VRM风扇设计对超频用户和高端平台散热尤为有利。未来若能在软件上放宽文件格式限制并增加更多预设主题，将进一步提升竞争力。

history.title: Intel's EMIB packaging gains traction as chip designers look to skirt TSMC's CoWoS constraints — Google's reported decision for 9th-gen TPUs highlights Intel's attractive alternative
history.description: Google has reportedly chosen Intel's EMIB-T over TSMC's CoWoS-L for its next-generation TPU, codenamed Humufish. But will Google be alone in its alleged decision?
history.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Google plans to use Intel's EMIB-T packaging for its next-generation TPU codenamed Humufish, according to SemiAnalysis. TSMC's portfolio of chip-on-wafer-on-substrate (CoWoS) technologies has become the de facto standard advanced packaging option for nearly all AI and HPC processors made in the industry. Competing offerings are usually considered as secondary solutions if CoWoS is in tight supply, but things are beginning to change.Google is a long-standing CoWoS customer for TPUs, starting from the Third-Generation TPU, all the way to Google's latest Eighth-Generation TPUs. Assuming that SemiAnalysis's report about Google's decision to move to EMIB-T with its Ninth-Generation TPUs is accurate, it's a big decision for Google, as switching from one advanced packaging technology to another is a complicated endeavor, which involves plenty of changes and unknowns. Understanding Google's reasons for the switch could shed some light on the prospects of Intel's and TSMC's advanced packaging technologies, which will be used by leading chip designers and hyperscalers in the coming years.Advanced packaging technologies at glanceFor years, Google used TSMC's CoWoS-S, and later, CoWoS-L packaging. Initially, the company used CoWoS-S packaging, which relies on a silicon interposer up to 3.3X the reticle size, but with its 7th- and 8th-Generation TPUs, the company moved to CoWoS-L. CoWoS-L relies on a redistribution layer (RDL) interposer with embedded local silicon interconnect (LSI) bridges that enable high-performance die-to-die links, which can scale packages to 5.5X the reticle size today. TSMC promises to improve CoWoS-L's capabilities to scale over 14X the reticle size by the end of the decade.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: (Image credit: Intel)Unlike CoWoS, Intel's embedded multi-die interconnect bridge (EMIB) technology does not use any interposers. The technology instead relies on tiny embedded silicon bridges within the substrate to enable high-density die-to-die interconnections, whereas everything else is routed through an inexpensive organic substrate.EMIB-T adds through-silicon vias (TSVs) to the bridge, which enables power to flow vertically instead of going through the organic substrate. In addition, Intel's EMIB-T also integrates sophisticated metal-insulator-metal (MIM) capacitors and a dedicated ground plane into the bridge to improve power integrity. The latter is a particularly important feature of complex next-generation AI accelerators, which demand more, cleaner power, and for which power delivery is becoming as challenging as signal routing. You may like Google reportedly books Intel for packaging more than 3 million TPUs in 2028 TSMC says panel packaging won't replace CoWoS anytime soon for the largest future AI processors TSMC's details next-gen CoWoS roadmap: over 14-reticle packages and 48x leap in compute power expected by 2029 The main selling point of EMIB (and EMIB-T) is that it is not constrained by interposer reticle limits as it places small silicon bridges only where high-density die-to-die links are needed. Strictly speaking, CoWoS-L is not either, as it uses LSIs locally as well. The difference is that those bridges are embedded into a package-wide RDL interposer that connects everything and enables dense interconnections across the package.Since both CoWoS-L and EMIB-T are designed to address the same applications and have many similarities in the way they do this, the choice between them is likely driven by a combination of factors rather than one single advantage or disadvantage. On the technology side of matters, these factors include interconnect performance and density, power delivery, scaling beyond very large package sizes, and mechanical rigidity. On the business side of things, costs, capacity availability, and supply chain diversification are also a significant factor.Crucial differences (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)SemiAnalysis claims that the main advantage of EMIB/EMIB-T over CoWoS is the lack of reticle limit, but this argument does not fully hold against CoWoS-L, as it was invented specifically to escape the reticle limitation by replacing the monolithic silicon interposer with localized LSI bridges.When it comes to dense, package-wide routing, CoWoS-L's RDL interposer is fundamentally superior to an ordinary organic substrate offered by EMIB-T. Organic substrate wiring has coarser line/space dimensions and larger vias, so it cannot provide the same routing density as CoWoS-L's fine-pitch RDL. Where an EMIB bridge connects adjacent dies, Intel can achieve very high interconnect density. But anything that needs to travel beyond those bridges must use the package substrate or cross a topology involving additional bridges.By contrast, CoWoS-L gives the designer two levels of connectivity: LSIs provide extremely dense local die-to-die connections, while the global RDL interposer provides relatively dense and flexible routing across the entire package. This means the RDL can carry longer, lower-density connections without consuming valuable LSI resources, while still offering much finer routing than the underlying package substrate. What to read next Analyzing TSMC's fab expansion roadmap — multi-fab N2 ramp, CoWoS, SoIC, and uncorking bottlenecks Inside Google's TPU V8 strategy, delivering two chips for two crucial tasks at incredible scale Intel, SK hynix shares surge following reports of chip packaging partnership — SK is said to be testing Intel's 2.5D EMIB for HBM integration One scenario for Google's choice is that it potentially wanted better power delivery than what CoWoS-L could offer. EMIB-T integrates TSVs for vertical power delivery, sophisticated MIM capacitors for local decoupling, and a dedicated ground plane into its silicon bridges. The combination of these features substantially reduces power-delivery impedance and improves transient response and power integrity, which gives EMIB-T a major advantage over conventional EMIB for power-hungry AI accelerators. However, we have no idea how EMIB-T stacks up against CoWoS-L in the case of Google’s Humufish.Of course, the larger the RDL interposer becomes, the greater its parasitics can become, potentially limiting scaling unless TSMC finds ways to mitigate them. However, EMIB does not eliminate long-distance wiring: If two distant dies must communicate, those signals still have to travel somewhere, and routing them through an organic substrate is not inherently electrically superior to routing them through a purpose-built RDL interposer. Therefore, it is difficult to claim that Google chose EMIB-T over CoWoS-L, simply because EMIB-T offers superior package-wide electrical characteristics.After Nvidia suffered yield loss with its Blackwell data center GPUs due to an alleged mismatch in the coefficient of thermal expansion (CTE) among the GPU chiplets, LSI bridges, RDL interposer, and motherboard substrate, which led to warping and system failure, it is reasonable to question the mechanical rigidity of CoWoS-L packages. Nvidia has found a solution for its dual compute chiplet Blackwell packages, and so have other developers of AI accelerators. However, as package dimensions increase, they may behave differently, therefore causing yield losses.By contrast, EMIB/EMIB-T eliminates the large RDL interposer and embeds small silicon bridges in the organic substrate, so most of the package consists of the substrate itself. This does not make EMIB/EMIB-T packages immune to mechanical failures, as large packages can warp and bend, causing various problems. However, as such packages lack the very source of global thermomechanical stress, they can potentially be more robust mechanically. However, EMIB-T can potentially complicate things because TSVs, additional metal structures, MIM capacitors, and their ground plane make the bridge more complex. Thus, Intel must manage both global package warpage and local stresses around each embedded bridge to ensure the mechanical rigidity of these packages.Ironically, while CoWoS-L can offer denser package-wide routing, which is better for ultra-large processors, EMIB-T may potentially provide better mechanical rigidity required for such devices. Nonetheless, EMIB-T and its organic substrate do not eliminate package bending or cracking risks entirely.EconomicsIf Google's Humufish TPU really moves to EMIB-T, the decision could well be both technical and strategic. Google has the engineering resources to opt for an all-new packaging technology in an effort to lower costs and eliminate dependence on TSMC's constrained CoWoS capacity. Nvidia tends to procure advanced packaging allocations years in advance, so it is possible that Google could simply not get enough CoWoS-L wafers for its 9th-generation TPU.As a bonus, Google can also build relationships with Intel Foundry without using the company's fabrication technologies. In fact, keeping in mind that Intel and Google already have a strategic agreement covering Intel Xeon CPUs, it wouldn't be too surprising to learn that the cloud giant is courting Intel Foundry as well.Both Intel's EMIB-T and TSMC's CoWoS-L have their own technological and economic advantages and disadvantages. Perhaps the biggest advantage of CoWoS-L is its predictability, as the company has experience with that tech. However, if Google has decided to drop that predictability in favor of an all-new packaging method, it may well have a combination of technological and strategic reasons to do so.
history.author: Anton Shilov
history.titleZh: 英特尔EMIB封装技术逐渐普及，芯片设计者欲规避台积电CoWoS限制——据称谷歌第九代TPU选用英特尔方案，凸显其替代吸引力
history.descriptionZh:
根据SemiAnalysis的最新报道，谷歌计划为其代号为Humufish的第九代TPU（张量处理单元）采用英特尔的EMIB-T先进封装技术。这一决定意义重大，因为自第三代TPU以来，谷歌一直是台积电CoWoS（芯片-晶圆-基板）封装技术的长期客户，从CoWoS-S演进至CoWoS-L。放弃成熟的CoWoS-L转而拥抱英特尔的新兴EMIB-T，意味着谷歌要面对技术切换带来的复杂性和不确定性，背后必然有深刻的技术和战略考量。

**核心技术与创新点对比**

要理解谷歌的选择，首先要厘清两大技术路径的本质差异。

**台积电CoWoS-L**：这是CoWoS家族的最新成员。其核心是一块覆盖整个封装区域的RDL（重分布层）中介层。在这块中介层中，嵌入了局部硅互连（LSI）桥，用于实现高端芯片间的极高密度互连。同时，整个RDL层本身也提供了比传统有机基板更精细的全局布线能力。这种“局部高密度桥接 + 全局精细互连”的双层结构，使得CoWoS-L在提供超高密度Die-to-Die（芯片间）连接的同时，也能灵活处理封装内远距离的信号路由。目前，CoWoS-L可支持高达5.5倍光罩尺寸的封装，台积电计划在2029年前将其扩展至14倍以上。

**英特尔EMIB-T**：EMIB（嵌入式多芯片互连桥）技术摒弃了笨重且成本高昂的大面积中介层。它只在有机基板内的特定位置嵌入微小的硅桥，用于连接那些需要高密度通信的相邻芯片，而其他所有信号都通过廉价、但布线较粗糙的有机基板进行。EMIB-T是EMIB的增强版，核心创新是在硅桥中集成了硅通孔（TSV）、高精度MIM（金属-绝缘体-金属）电容和专用接地层。其中，TSV允许电力垂直通过硅桥传输，而非绕道有机基板，这对于功耗极高的AI加速器至关重要。MIM电容和接地层则显著改善了电源完整性，能够提供更纯净、更稳定的电力，这是应对下一代AI芯片巨大功耗挑战的关键。

**性能参数与核心差异**

虽然两者都旨在解决超大尺寸AI芯片的封装问题，但各有优劣：

1.  **全局布线密度**：这是CoWoS-L的强项。它的RDL层具有比EMIB-T有机基板更精细的线宽/线距，能实现封装内更密集、更灵活的全局布线。而在EMIB-T中，超出硅桥连接范围的信号必须通过较粗糙的有机基板，布线密度受限。简言之，CoWoS-L提供“高密度局部连接 + 中等密度全局连接”的双层网络，而EMIB-T则只有“高密度局部连接 + 低密度全局连接”。

2.  **电源传输与完整性**：这是EMIB-T的突出优势。通过在桥内集成TSV、MIM电容和接地层，EMIB-T能将电源直接垂直输送给计算核心，大幅降低供电阻抗，优化瞬态响应，从而提供远优于传统封装方式的电源质量。虽然CoWoS-L也在不断演进，但在电源完整性这个AI芯片的热点问题上，EMIB-T的设计天生更具竞争力。这也是谷歌可能看中的关键点之一。

3.  **机械鲁棒性（可靠性）**：这是业界非常关注的一点。英伟达的Blackwell GPU曾因CoWoS封装中多种材料（芯片、LSI桥、RDL中介层、主板基板）的热膨胀系数（CTE）不匹配，导致翘曲和系统失效。随着封装尺寸越来越大，这种风险会加剧。相比之下，EMIB/EMIB-T去除了大面积RDL中介层，封装主要由有机基板构成，减少了热机械应力的根源，理论上机械鲁棒性更强。但另一方面，EMIB-T的硅桥本身结构更复杂（集成TSV、电容等），可能引入局部应力点。因此，两者的机械可靠性之争尚无定论。

**技术影响与应用场景**

如果消息属实，选择EMIB-T对谷歌而言是一个技术和战略并重的决策。

从**技术层面**看，Humufish作为下一代AI加速器，功耗和性能密度必然激增。EMIB-T在电源完整性方面的优势，可能刚好解决了CoWoS-L在极大规模下供电的瓶颈。同时，随着封装尺寸接近或超过光罩极限，CoWoS-L中介层的寄生效应可能变得严重，而EMIB-T由于没有大面积中介层，理论上更易扩展。此外，EMIB-T潜在的机械优势也是关键考量。

从**战略和经济层面**看，原因可能更为直接：**供应链多元化与成本控制**。台积电的CoWoS产能长期供不应求，英伟达等大客户会提前数年预订产能。谷歌或许无法获得足够的CoWoS产能来支撑其第九代TPU的巨大需求量。转向英特尔，不仅能确保产能，还能与英特尔代工服务（IFS）建立合作关系，而不必使其制造自家芯片。考虑到谷歌和英特尔已有Xeon服务器芯片的战略合作，此举显得顺理成章。同时，多供应商策略能增强议价能力、降低成本。

**结论**

英特尔EMIB-T和台积电CoWoS-L各有千秋，并非简单的技术优劣之分。CoWoS-L的优势在于其成熟度、可预测性以及全局布线密度，对超大规模处理器依然很有吸引力。EMIB-T则在电源交付完整性、潜在的机械可靠性以及供应链多元化上提供了独特价值。谷歌选择冒险切换，很可能是基于其庞大的工程资源，综合权衡了技术性能（特别是供电）、产能安全、成本以及战略合作伙伴关系后的结果。这一决策不仅关乎Humufish的成败，更预示着未来AI芯片封装技术路线的风向变化——它标志着英特尔在先进封装领域正成为台积电一个强有力的、具有独特技术特色的竞争者。

history.title: Palit officially announces RTX 3060 return with 'new' Infinity 2 OC launch — 2021 GPU with 12GB of VRAM is an AI crisis stopgap
history.description: Nvidia has rebooted its five-year-old RTX 3060 graphics card, as is, for the modern AI era, bringing back the GPU officially at its original $329 price. It still features 12GB of VRAM, which serves as its main selling point during a memory crisis, but don't let the capacity fool you.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 7 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Nvidia has just relaunched its five-year-old graphics card, the GeForce RTX 3060, in order to combat the component scarcity driven by AI. This resurrection has seen a staggered release, with companies like Gigabyte and Manli silently listing their renewed variants in different parts of the world. Today, Palit joined the list with an official announcement for its RTX 3060 Infinity 2 OC, the first official indication of Ampere's return.A post shared by Palit Global (@palit_global)A photo posted by on If we take a look at the specs, the card is identical to the original RTX 3060 we saw in 2021 — it has 3,584 CUDA cores paired with 12GB of GDDR6 VRAM saturated across a 192-bit-wide bus. Since this is an overclocked variant, it can boost up to 1,792 MHz, which is less than 1% higher than the base 1,777 MHz boost that Nvidia already mandates. Palit will also release a non-OC variant of the Infinity 2 RTX 3060.The card features a relatively simple design characterized by the typical black aesthetic we see on budget GPUs. There's no zero-RPM tech here, but Palit claims 0dB noise levels and says there's a "protective backplate" on the card that prevents PCB flex. When you're buying a GPU like this, its looks are probably the least of your concern; the main selling point is, of course, that 12GB memory pool.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Image 1 of 3(Image credit: Palit)(Image credit: Palit)(Image credit: Palit)Late last year, the PC hardware industry entered one of its most turbulent eras, characterized by production inadequacies created by the AI boom. Artificial intelligence demands a lot of memory and storage, and thus, commodity silicon has skyrocketed in price overnight with no signs of slowing down. After evading the crisis initially, GPUs eventually got wrapped up in the price hikes as well.Nvidia's latest Blackwell family uses cutting-edge GDDR7 memory, which is even more expensive, and since vendors are busy producing HBM for fatter margins instead, the "solution" to this dilemma had to be creative. The idea for reviving older generation cards was actually floated by our very own Paul Alcorn at a Q&A at CES 2026, where Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang replied, saying he'd "go back and take a look at this." You may like Legacy Nvidia RTX 3060 12GB returns to retail five years after original launch, priced at $339 Memory famine compels GPU vendors to re-release 2020 graphics cards Nvidia RTX 3060 comeback in 2026 could alleviate soaring GPU prices and memory shortages Palit hasn't provided a price for the Infinity 2 OC yet, and we didn't see it listed on retailers yet, but we can infer it'll cost around $329 given similar variants have also been popping up for that price. To be clear, the RTX 3060 is a great value GPU that we praised even back when it launched. But in the face of the RTX 50-series, it's not exactly something you should consider first, especially when buying new.You can get an RTX 5060 Ti for just $369 right now on Newegg, which is a significantly faster card with support for DLSS 4.5 and multi-frame gen, not to mention the better efficiency. It only has 8GB of VRAM, unfortunately, which hurts performance at high resolutions and if you like to play with ray tracing enabled. Still, unless you're going up to 4K, the 5060 Ti (and the regular 5060) will perform much better than a 3060 no matter what, as you can see in our GPU benchmark hierarchy.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsUltimately, rebooting the RTX 3060 makes sense from a manufacturer's point of view given the better yields of Samsung's older 8nm process compared to TSMC's more expensive N5 node used by Ada and Blackwell. But as a consumer, you should definitely stick with the RTX 50-series or try to find a used RTX 40-series GPU instead. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Hassam Nasir
history.titleZh: Palit officially announces RTX 3060 return with 'new' Infinity 2 OC launch — 2021 GPU with 12GB of VRAM is an AI crisis stopgap
history.descriptionZh:
英伟达近期重新发布了五年前的老款显卡GeForce RTX 3060，以应对AI热潮导致的组件短缺问题。此次回归采用了分区域、分批上市的策略，此前技嘉和万丽已在全球不同市场悄然上架了更新版本。今天，耕升（Palit）正式发布了RTX 3060 Infinity 2 OC超频版，这是Ampere架构回归的首个官方信号。

从规格上看，该显卡与2021年原版RTX 3060完全一致：搭载3584个CUDA核心、12GB GDDR6显存，位宽192-bit。由于是超频版，其加速频率可达1792 MHz，仅比英伟达默认的1777 MHz加速频率高出不到1%。此外，耕升还将推出非超频版Infinity 2。显卡设计简洁，采用入门级显卡常见的黑色外观，不支持零转速风扇技术，但耕升宣称可实现0dB噪音水平，并配备了保护背板以防止PCB弯曲。对于这类显卡，外观并非重点，核心卖点仍是12GB大显存。

去年年底，PC硬件行业因AI浪潮进入动荡期。AI对内存和存储需求极大，导致普通硅材料价格一夜飙升且无回落迹象。显卡最初逃过一劫，但最终也未能幸免于涨价潮。英伟达最新的Blackwell系列采用昂贵的GDDR7显存，而厂商们更倾向于生产高利润的HBM内存。面对这一困境，英伟达不得不采取创造性解决方案。实际上，这个复活旧卡的创意源自Tom's Hardware编辑Paul Alcorn在2026年CES上的一次问答，当时英伟达CEO黄仁勋回应称会“回去研究一下”。

耕升尚未公布Infinity 2 OC的售价，零售渠道也未见上架。参考已出现的类似版本，预计价格约为329美元。需要明确的是，RTX 3060是一款性价比出色的显卡，即便在2021年发布时也备受好评。但面对RTX 50系列，它并非新购首选。目前新蛋上RTX 5060 Ti售价仅369美元，性能大幅领先，支持DLSS 4.5和多帧生成，能效更优。不过遗憾的是，它只有8GB显存，在高分辨率和开启光追时性能受限。尽管如此，除非玩家使用4K分辨率，否则RTX 5060 Ti甚至标准版5060的性能无论如何都远超RTX 3060（可参考Tom's Hardware的GPU基准测试排行）。

从制造商角度看，复活RTX 3060是合理的：三星8nm老工艺的良率优于台积电用于Ada和Blackwell的昂贵N5节点。但对消费者而言，建议优先考虑RTX 50系列，或寻找二手RTX 40系列显卡。

history.title: Intel's big $5 billion bet on Ireland aims to right the wrongs of the cancelled Magdeburg, Germany complex — Fab 34's proven pipeline and Intel 3 node should help the company meet insatiable HPC demand
history.description: The announcement comes just shy of a year after CEO Lip-Bu Tan cancelled Intel's planned €30 billion fab complex in Germany and a €4.6 billion assembly and test plant in Poland.
history.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Intel announced a €5 billion ($5.7 billion) investment on Monday to expand chip production at its Leixlip campus in County Kildare, Ireland, upgrading existing fabs to increase output of Intel 3 wafers for Xeon 6 and next-gen server processors. The program accounts for roughly 30% of Intel's planned 2026 capital expenditure of about $17 billion, adds several hundred permanent roles to a 4,900-strong Irish workforce, and is scheduled to be substantially deployed by the end of 2027. Naga Chandrasekaran, Intel's chief technology and operations officer and general manager of Intel Foundry, told Reuters that "the demand for servers, the demand for AI is driving a significant increase in the need for Intel 3 wafers."The announcement comes just shy of a year after CEO Lip-Bu Tan cancelled Intel's planned €30 billion fab complex in Magdeburg, Germany, and a €4.6 billion assembly and test plant in Wrocław, Poland. Those cancellations came with a memo in which Tan wrote that Intel had "invested too much, too soon – without adequate demand."The Ireland program, however, passes the test Magdeburg failed on every measure that Intel boss Lip-bu Tan set: It uses cleanrooms that already exist; it's funded from Intel's own capex with no announced state aid; and it expands an already shipping revenue product into a demand pipeline Intel says currently exceeds its supply.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: What €5 billion buys (Image credit: Intel)It’s understood that no new manufacturing plants are part of the program, with the money instead going to upgrades of existing fab facilities, installation of leading-edge production equipment, and an expansion of the automated track system that links the campus's manufacturing modules into a single production flow. Intel said the work began earlier this year and will employ around 2,000 specialized tradespeople during the build-out, on top of the permanent hires.Fab 34 is the focal point of the spending, with Chandrasekaran telling the Irish Times that "Ireland is our centre of excellence for Intel 3; we are not running Intel 3 in any other Intel manufacturing facilities." The fab began high-volume production on Intel 4 in September 2023, as the first EUV facility in Europe, and it now runs both Intel 4 and Intel 3, producing compute tiles for Core Ultra parts and Xeon 6 server processors. Intel has spent more than €30 billion in Ireland since 1989, over half of it between 2019 and 2023, doubling the campus's manufacturing footprint. You may like Intel invests $5.7 billion in Ireland fab Intel's fab roadmap examined — Arizona, Ohio, Ireland, and the two deadlines deciding 14A process node Google reportedly books Intel for packaging more than 3 million TPUs in 2028 The spending follows directly from a transaction Intel closed in April, when it bought back the 49% stake in the Fab 34 joint venture it had sold to Apollo-managed funds for $11.2 billion in 2024, paying $14.2 billion to reclaim it. Apollo walked away with a roughly 27% gain in under two years. Intel now owns 100% of every wafer Leixlip produces, so each additional Intel 3 wafer the €5 billion generates flows entirely to Intel's own margin, rather than being shared with an outside capital partner.The projects Intel cancelledMagdeburg had roughly €9.9 billion in pledged German subsidies attached when Tan killed it, and Wroclaw had €1.9 billion in approved EU state aid. Fab 38 in Kiryat Gat, Israel, remains paused, and the Ohio site has slipped to around 2030. Every leading-edge wafer Intel produces for the foreseeable future comes from three U.S. states and one Irish campus, a concentration that made Leixlip the only European site left to expand and the cheapest place anywhere in Intel's network to add advanced capacity quickly, since the shells, EUV tools, and workforce are already in place.Intel's Data Center and AI revenue rose 22% year over year to $5.1 billion in Q1 2026, and CFO David Zinsner told analysts on the April earnings call that Intel faces "unprecedented demand for silicon," with demand exceeding supply across the company's server lines. Intel Foundry revenue grew 16% to $5.4 billion in the same quarter, but external foundry revenue was just $174 million against a $2.4 billion operating loss, so the wafers that pay for Leixlip's tools are overwhelmingly Intel's own Xeon chips, rather than customer designs. A single campus running the entirety of a revenue-critical node also concentrates risk: Any disruption at Leixlip has no second source, because Intel 3 exists nowhere else.Europe's most advanced node, without European money (Image credit: Intel)Intel 3 is now the most advanced process technology manufactured anywhere in Europe, and Chandrasekaran told the Irish Times the expansion provides "a technology sovereignty within the EU that the EU is targeting." Interestingly, no EU or Irish state aid accompanied the announcement, which distinguishes it from TSMC's €10 billion ESMC fab in Dresden, where the European Commission approved a €5 billion funding package for a plant producing 28/22nm and 16/12nm chips for automotive and industrial customers, scheduled to be operational from late 2027. Europe's only leading-edge logic production is self-funded by an American company for its own products, while its subsidized flagship project makes trailing-edge silicon.Commercial electricity in Ireland runs up to twice the rates Intel pays in Arizona or Taiwan. Intel warned Irish ministers in August 2025 that its competitiveness was under threat from energy costs, and the company flagged up to 195 mandatory redundancies at Leixlip in mid-2025 as part of its global workforce reduction. IDA Ireland paid Intel €30 million in 2023 to offset elevated EU power bills, so that "self-funded" framing has at least one recent caveat.Meanwhile, Intel's 14A node is being developed in Oregon; no Irish role in it has been announced, and two prospective 14A customers will decide the node's fate in commitments expected between late 2026 and early 2027. Ultimately, the €5 billion makes Leixlip the fully-loaded workhorse of a node Intel will eventually move past, not a contender for the leading edge. Whether Europe gets anything newer than Intel 3 remains to be seen.
history.author: Luke James
history.titleZh: 英特尔在爱尔兰的50亿美元豪赌旨在纠正德国马格德堡被取消的工厂的错误——Fab 34经过验证的产品线以及Intel 3制程节点应能帮助公司满足对高性能计算永无止境的需求
history.descriptionZh:
英特尔周一宣布在爱尔兰基尔代尔郡莱克斯利普园区投资50亿欧元（约合57亿美元）扩建芯片产能，通过升级现有晶圆厂来增加Intel 3工艺晶圆的产量，主要用于Xeon 6及下一代服务器处理器。这笔投资约占英特尔2026年计划资本支出（约170亿美元）的30%，将在现有4900人爱尔兰团队基础上增加数百个永久岗位，预计在2027年底前基本完成部署。英特尔首席技术官兼英特尔代工业务总经理Naga Chandrasekaran对路透社表示：“服务器需求和AI需求正推动对Intel 3晶圆的需求大幅增长。”

这一宣布距离英特尔CEO陈立武取消德国马格德堡价值300亿欧元的晶圆厂计划以及波兰弗罗茨瓦夫价值46亿欧元的组装与测试厂计划仅约一年。当时陈立武在备忘录中写道，英特尔“投资过多、过快，却没有足够的需求”。然而，爱尔兰项目通过了陈立武设定的所有测试：它利用已有的洁净室；资金来自英特尔自身资本支出，未宣布任何国家援助；它扩大的是一款已在出货的产品，且需求管道目前超过供应。

据了解，该项目不涉及新建制造工厂，资金将用于升级现有晶圆厂设施、安装尖端生产设备，以及扩建连接园区各制造模块的自动化轨道系统，使其形成单一生产流程。英特尔表示工作已于今年早些时候开始，建设期间将雇佣约2000名专业技工，此外还有永久招聘。Fab 34是本次投资的重点。Chandrasekaran告诉《爱尔兰时报》：“爱尔兰是我们Intel 3的卓越中心；我们没有在其他任何英特尔制造工厂运行Intel 3。”该晶圆厂于2023年9月开始大规模生产Intel 4工艺，成为欧洲首家EUV（极紫外光刻）工厂，目前同时运行Intel 4和Intel 3工艺，生产Core Ultra处理器和Xeon 6服务器处理器的计算模块。自1989年以来，英特尔在爱尔兰累计投资超过300亿欧元，其中一半以上是在2019年至2023年间投入，使园区制造面积扩大了一倍。

这笔支出直接源于英特尔今年4月完成的一笔交易——它从Apollo管理的基金手中回购了Fab 34合资企业49%的股份，支付了142亿美元（此前2024年以112亿美元出售）。Apollo在不到两年内获得了约27%的收益。英特尔现在完全拥有莱克斯利普生产的每一片晶圆，因此这50亿欧元产生的每一片额外Intel 3晶圆都将完全贡献给英特尔的自身利润，而非与外部资本伙伴分享。

对比英特尔取消的项目：马格德堡原本有约99亿欧元的德国补贴承诺，弗罗茨瓦夫有19亿欧元的欧盟国家援助批准。以色列基尔亚特盖特的Fab 38仍处于暂停状态，俄亥俄州工厂已推迟至2030年左右。在可预见的未来，英特尔生产的每一片尖端晶圆都来自美国三个州和爱尔兰这一个园区。这种集中度使莱克斯利普成为欧洲唯一可扩建的站点，也是英特尔全球网络中快速增加先进产能最便宜的地方，因为厂房、EUV设备和劳动力都已到位。

2026年第一季度，英特尔数据中心和AI收入同比增长22%至51亿美元。首席财务官David Zinsner在4月财报电话会议上告诉分析师，英特尔面临“前所未有的硅需求”，服务器产品线的需求超过供应。同期，英特尔代工业务收入增长16%至54亿美元，但外部代工收入仅为1.74亿美元，运营亏损24亿美元。因此，为莱克斯利普设备买单的晶圆绝大多数是英特尔自家的Xeon芯片，而非客户设计。一个园区承担整个营收关键节点的全部产量也集中了风险：莱克斯利普的任何中断都没有第二来源，因为Intel 3工艺不存在于其他地方。

Intel 3现在是欧洲制造的最先进工艺技术。Chandrasekaran告诉《爱尔兰时报》，此次扩建提供了“欧盟所追求的技术主权”。有趣的是，该宣布并未伴随欧盟或爱尔兰的国家援助，这与台积电在德累斯顿的100亿欧元ESMC晶圆厂形成对比——欧盟委员会批准了50亿欧元资助计划，用于生产28/22nm和16/12nm汽车和工业芯片，预计2027年底投产。欧洲唯一的尖端逻辑生产是由一家美国公司为其自身产品自筹资金，而其受补贴的旗舰项目却在制造落后制程芯片。

爱尔兰的商业电价是英特尔在亚利桑那州或台湾支付的两倍。2025年8月，英特尔警告爱尔兰部长，其竞争力正受到能源成本的威胁，并在2025年中期在莱克斯利普计划裁减最多195个岗位（作为全球裁员一部分）。爱尔兰投资发展局（IDA Ireland）在2023年向英特尔支付了3000万欧元以抵消高昂的欧盟电费，因此“自筹资金”的说法至少有一个近期例外。

与此同时，英特尔的14A节点正在俄勒冈州开发；尚未宣布其在爱尔兰的角色，两个潜在的14A客户将在2026年底至2027年初之间以承诺决定该节点的命运。最终，这50亿欧元将使莱克斯利普成为英特尔最终将超越的节点的满载主力，而不是争夺最前沿的地位。欧洲能否获得比Intel 3更新的工艺，仍有待观察。

history.title: NVIDIA and Japan Bring Full-Stack AI and Robotics to Every Industry
history.description: Home to leading manufacturers, robotics pioneers, infrastructure builders and iconic gaming companies, of course, Japan is one of the world’s centers of AI — building across the full stack with NVIDIA technologies. This week NVIDIA and its partners in Japan are showcasing the AI ecosystem’s latest advancements. Check back here for updates.
history.content: NVIDIA and SEGA Celebrate 30 Years of Innovation, Bringing ‘VIRTUA FIGHTER CROSSROADS’ and Other Legendary SEGA Games to NVIDIA RTX Spark 🔗 NVIDIA and SEGA are celebrating more than three decades of collaboration by bringing VIRTUA FIGHTER CROSSROADS and future SEGA titles to NVIDIA RTX Spark — a new superchip for slim Windows laptops and compact desktop PCs. This builds on the companies’ long-standing relationship, which began 30 years ago when NVIDIA worked with SEGA on burgeoning graphics technology for arcade systems and gaming consoles — with the NVIDIA NV1 chip powering the first Virtua Fighter title on PC, among the world’s first 3D fighting games. SEGA will support RTX Spark, giving gamers new ways to experience SEGA’s iconic franchises, including the upcoming VIRTUA FIGHTER CROSSROADS. Announced from the heart of Akihabara, a global gaming technology hub, at the original SEGA Akihabara Arcade (now GiGO Akihabara 3), VIRTUA FIGHTER CROSSROADS coming to RTX Spark reinforces the companies’ commitment to innovation and shows a glimpse of the future of gaming on a new era of Windows PCs designed for personal agents, AI, creating and gaming. NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang joined SEGA CEO Haruki Satomi; SEGA chief operating officer Shuji Utsumi; Yu Suzuki, creator of Virtua Fighter; and former SEGA President Shoichiro Irimajiri, at the birthplace of countless arcade memories to celebrate the milestone. They showcased how technology partnerships can evolve across generations of hardware and software, connecting the gaming industry’s heritage with its future. The expanding NVIDIA RTX Spark ecosystem — including SEGA and other industry leaders — will offer gamers new experiences harnessing NVIDIA ray tracing, DLSS and AI technologies, while preserving and celebrating the iconic franchises they know and love. Learn more about NVIDIA RTX Spark.
history.author: NVIDIA Writers
history.titleZh: 英伟达与日本携手，为各行各业带来全栈AI与机器人技术
history.descriptionZh:
英伟达与世嘉（SEGA）共同庆祝双方长达三十余年的创新合作关系，宣布将《VR战士 十字路口》（VIRTUA FIGHTER CROSSROADS）等经典游戏系列引入英伟达全新推出的RTX Spark超级芯片平台。该平台专为轻薄型Windows笔记本电脑和紧凑型台式电脑设计，旨在开启个人AI、创作与游戏的新纪元。这一合作根植于深厚的历史渊源：三十年前，英伟达与世嘉在街机系统和游戏主机的图形技术领域展开合作，其首款图形芯片NV1正是为PC版《VR战士》提供动力——这款游戏也是世界上最早的3D格斗游戏之一。

在东京秋叶原——全球游戏科技的心脏地带，原世嘉秋叶原街机厅（现GiGO秋叶原3号馆）内，英伟达创始人兼CEO黄仁勋与世嘉CEO里见治纪、COO内海修二、《VR战士》创作者铃木裕、前世嘉社长入交昭一郎共同见证了这一里程碑时刻。他们展示了技术合作伙伴关系如何跨越硬件与软件的代际更迭，连接游戏产业的过去与未来。

RTX Spark生态系统的持续扩展，包括世嘉及其他行业领军者的加入，将为玩家带来前所未有的游戏体验：借助英伟达的光线追踪（Ray Tracing）、深度学习超级采样（DLSS）以及AI技术，在保留并致敬经典游戏系列的同时，实现画质与性能的飞跃。即将登陆RTX Spark的《VR战士 十字路口》正是这一愿景的集中体现——它不仅是世嘉标志性IP的重生，更标志着游戏行业从传统硬件向融合个人智能代理、AI计算与高性能游戏的新一代Windows PC平台转型的关键一步。

history.title: CXMT close to matching Micron's memory capacity in 2026, research claims — would put China on track to become world's second-largest DRAM producer
history.description: As CXMT's DRAM capacity set to match Micron's, China's DRAM industry could become world's second largest after South Korea.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter ChangXin Memory Technologies (CXMT), China's largest DRAM maker, is on track to match Micron's production capacity in 2026, if Citrini Research's forecasting models are correct. If this happens, China will become the world's second-largest DRAM production base in the coming years.Go deeper with TH Premium: Memory (Image credit: SK Hynix)AI data centers are swallowing the world's memory and storage supplyThe future of DRAM: From DDR5 to future ICsHigh-bandwidth memory roadmapHere's why HBM is coming for your PC's RAMThe bottom-up model estimates that CXMT will finish 2026 with approximately 350,000 wafer starts per month (WSPM) of DRAM capacity, which is just 25,000 WPM less than Micron. According to the analysis, the federal government is pushing CXMT to share its DRAM technology with JHICC, Swaysure, and YMTC's subsidiary XMC to ease domestic shortages. All three companies have either built DRAM capacity already, or will do so in the short-term future, the report claims.Swaysure has completed construction of a 140,000-WSPM fab in Shenzhen, while JHICC's Jinjiang complex contains enough cleanroom space for 120,000 WSPM, and the initial 60,000-WSPM phase is expected to receive equipment by the end of 2026. YMTC is also projected to operate about 50,000 WSPM of DRAM production at Wuhan Fab 3. If all these facilities initiate operations in the coming years, then China will have a total DRAM capacity of 600,000 WSPM (not counting Samsung's and SK hynix's fabs in China), which is dramatically lower compared to South Korea, but ahead of Japan, Taiwan, and the U.S. combined.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: But China is not going to stop developing its DRAM industry, and by 2030, its total capacity will increase to around 1.41 million WSPM, according to Citrini. CXMT alone is projected to build new production capacities in Beijing, Hefei, and Shanghai, to expand its production capability to 950,000 WSPM in 2030, assuming everything goes as planned.The supply model assumes that about 400,000 WSPM of CXMT output will remain on D1a, another 400,000 WSPM will migrate to D1b, and roughly 150,000 WPM will produce D1c devices. You may like Chinese memory brands ditch Samsung and Micron for homegrown CXMT and YMTC silicon CXMT enters mainstream consumer supply chain with Corsair Vengeance DDR5 kit Micron's Virginia fab begins producing America's most advanced DRAM memory Swipe to scroll horizontallyForecasted DRAM manufacturing capacities (in thousands WSPM)Row 0 - Cell 0 2026E2027E - 2029E2030ECXMT350?950JHICC-60120Micron375??Samsung720?1,140 - 1,450SK hynix590?1,180Swaysure-?140YMTC/XMC5050200Enough fab tools?Citrini admits that producing China's outlook is considerably more difficult than predicting the development of established DRAM makers. On the one hand, there is rapidly expanding fabrication infrastructure in China, abundant state-backed financing, and government-directed technology transfers. On the other hand, among the key near-term limitations remains lithography equipment availability, particularly if the proposed MATCH Act restricts sales of advanced immersion DUV tools to select Chinese companies.However, the author expects SMEE's domestic immersion DUV scanners to enter volume production around late 2026 or early 2027 following beta testing, as well as SiCarrier/Yuliangsheng introduce its own production-ready DUV platform in 2028. Perhaps a bit optimistically, the analysts predict that availability of lithography tools is not expected to constrain Chinese production beyond 2028, at least for mature logic and DRAM nodes. Yet, for obvious reasons, if the MATCH Act works as planned and disrupts supply of advanced immersion DUV tools to DRAM makers, production capacity expansions will not occur in the next couple of years, the report suggests.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsStill, both SMEE and SiCarrier will need time to ramp up production of their lithography systems, whereas DRAM makers must learn how to use them efficiently, so we would not be as optimistic as the authors and would not expect Chinese tools to produce meaningful DRAM volumes before the early 2030s. Still, the key takeaway here is that China is on track to become a major DRAM maker rather sooner than later.Unprecedented demandCitrini Research projects total DRAM demand to reach 157.5 exabytes (EB) per year by 2030, including 75 EB of commodity DRAM for agentic AI CPUs, 25 EB of commodity DRAM for conventional cloud servers, 20 EB of commodity DRAM for client devices, and 37.5 EB of HBM4E as well as HBM5 for AI accelerators (15 EB and 22.5 EB, respectively).Meanwhile, Citrini expects the whole industry to only produce around 37.5 EB of HBM4E/HBM4 memory (mostly by Micron, Samsung, and SK hynix) as well as 91.3 EB of commodity DRAM (including output in China) in 2030, leaving a deficit of 28.7 EB, or roughly 25%.That said, the rapid expansion of DRAM production in China could be the industry's best hope to maintain relatively low prices of memory, something that will be particularly beneficial for the market of consumer devices that are sensitive to memory prices, analysts from Citrini believe. Yet, the author argues that most of this new capacity would satisfy China's own demand rather than eliminate the global shortage. Furthermore, even if companies like CXMT can expand their fabs faster, that additional capacity will mostly be consumed by domestic needs, according to Citrini.It should be noted that to make more memory, DRAM makers need more fab tools, primarily 193nm immersion scanners. Yet, companies like ASML, Canon, and Nikon cannot increase output of immersion DUV systems quickly as these are extremely complex machines containing tens of thousands of parts. While Chinese memory companies certainly pin their hopes on local producers like SMEE and SiCarrier, neither has delivered a single commercial immersion system, and after they do, it will take them years to ramp up production of such tools. history.author: Anton Shilov
history.titleZh: 研究称：长鑫存储2026年将接近美光存储容量——有望使中国成为全球第二大DRAM生产商
history.descriptionZh:
根据芯智研究（Citrini Research）的最新预测模型，中国最大的DRAM制造商长鑫存储（CXMT）有望在2026年与美光（Micron）的产能持平。若这一预测成真，中国将在未来几年成为全球第二大DRAM生产基地。这一判断基于自下而上的模型分析：到2026年底，CXMT的DRAM产能将达到约每月35万片晶圆（WSPM），仅比美光少2.5万片。更引人注目的是，中国正通过政府推动的技术共享加速国产化——联邦政府正敦促CXMT将其DRAM技术转让给晋华集成电路（JHICC）、昇维旭（Swaysure）以及长江存储（YMTC）的子公司XMC，以缓解国内短缺。

具体产能布局方面，昇维旭已在深圳建成一座14万WSPM的晶圆厂；晋华在晋江的综合体拥有12万WSPM的洁净室空间，其首期6万WSPM产线预计在2026年底接收设备；YMTC也计划在武汉Fab 3运营约5万WSPM的DRAM产能。若以上项目在数年内全部投产，中国DRAM总产能将达到60万WSPM（不计三星和SK海力士在华工厂），虽远低于韩国，但已超过日本、台湾和美国的总和。而芯智研究进一步预测，到2030年，中国DRAM总产能将增至约141万WSPM，其中仅CXMT就计划在北京、合肥和上海新建产线，目标产能达到95万WSPM。

从制程技术演进看，CXMT的产能分配将呈现明确的代际迁移：约40万WSPM保留在D1a节点（类似三星美光的1a级），另外40万WSPM迁移至D1b，约15万WSPM用于生产D1c器件。这一规划反映了中国DRAM行业在追赶先进制程时的务实策略——优先保证成熟节点的大规模量产，同时向更先进制程逐步过渡。

然而，产能扩张的最大瓶颈在于光刻设备供应。芯智研究坦言，预测中国DRAM前景比分析成熟厂商困难得多：一方面，中国拥有快速扩张的晶圆厂基础设施、充裕的国家背景融资以及政府导向的技术转移；另一方面，近期关键制约因素仍是光刻机可用性，尤其若美国《匹配法案》（MATCH Act）限制先进浸没式DUV光刻机对特定中国企业的销售，扩张计划将受严重影响。不过，报告乐观预计，上海微电子（SMEE）的国产浸没式DUV扫描仪将在2026年底或2027年初进入量产，而新凯莱/御粮生（SiCarrier/Yuliangsheng）计划在2028年推出其量产级DUV平台。分析师认为，到2028年后，至少对于成熟逻辑和DRAM节点，光刻工具的可获得性不会再制约中国生产——但前提是《匹配法案》不会如预期般干扰先进DUV设备的供应。即便国产光刻机成功下线，SMEE和SiCarrier仍需时间爬坡产能，而DRAM制造商也需要学习如何高效使用这些新设备。因此，更审慎的估计是：国产设备要到2030年代初才能产生有意义的DRAM产量。但核心结论不变：中国成为主要DRAM制造商的进程正在加速。

在需求端，芯智研究预测到2030年全球DRAM总需求将达到每年157.5艾字节（EB），其中包括：75 EB用于AI代理CPU的通用DRAM、25 EB用于传统云服务器、20 EB用于客户端设备，以及37.5 EB用于AI加速器的高带宽内存（HBM4E和HBM5分别为15 EB和22.5 EB）。而同期全球供应能力预计为：HBM4E/HBM4约37.5 EB（主要由美光、三星和SK海力士提供），通用DRAM约91.3 EB（含中国产能），总供应缺口达28.7 EB（约25%）。换言之，即使中国快速扩产，仍不足以填补全球短缺——分析师强调，中国新增产能大部分将优先满足国内需求而非出口。

这种供需失衡对市场意味着什么？芯智研究认为，中国DRAM产能的快速扩张是维持内存价格相对较低的“最好希望”，尤其对价格敏感的消费电子市场利好。但现实挑战同样严峻：要生产更多内存，需要更多制造工具，尤其是193nm浸没式光刻机。然而ASML、佳能、尼康等供应商无法快速提升这些包含数万个精密部件的复杂系统的产量。中国内存厂商将希望寄托于上海微电子和新凯莱等本土供应商，但目前两者均未交付任何商用浸没式系统——即便交付，爬坡也需要数年时间。

综上所述，中国正以政府主导+技术共享+国产设备替代的组合策略加速DRAM自主化。短期（2026年前）产能追赶主要依赖现有成熟制程和部分进口设备；中期（2028-2030年）则需验证国产光刻机的实际量产能力。若一切顺利，中国将在2030年形成约141万WSPM的DRAM总产能，成为全球仅次于韩国的第二大DRAM生产力量。这一进程不仅将深刻改变全球内存供应链格局，也将为AI数据中心、消费电子和云计算等下游行业带来更稳定的供应和更具竞争力的价格——前提是中美技术博弈不出现意外中断。

history.title: US gov't allows Chinese telecom giant ZTE to purchase Nvidia H200 AI chips — firm joins Alibaba, Tencent, and ByteDance in access to Hopper tech
history.description: The United States has licensed Chinese telecom giant ZTE to purchase restricted Nvidia H200 AI chips, but Chinese regulators and domestic procurement initiatives may limit the material impact of the change.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter The Sino-American chip wars have resulted in many back-and-forth salvos and negotiations as the countries try and strike a balance between technology access and trade. Currently, both sides have set respective import and export controls, letting specific companies on a case-by-case basis. Today, Reuters reports that Chinese telecoms giant ZTE and server firm Maginfra have received U.S. approval to buy Nvidia's last-gen H200 "Hopper" chips.ZTE joins a club that counts Alibaba, Tencent, ByteDance, and JD.com among the roughly 10-strong group of Chinese companies with U.S. clearance for those purchases. Additionally, an apparent subsidiary of Kingsoft Cloud got approval to buy AMD accelerators equivalent to Nvidia's H200, presumably Instinct MI300X-class chips.Over on the Chinese side of the table, Reuters remarks that there's no word on whether the respective authorities will give ZTE the go-ahead for import, as the country has taken on a protectionist stance as it tries to grow its own chip industry. The country has discouraged firms from purchasing foreign tech and has instead pushed companies to acquire homegrown accelerators. Huawei in particular has made great strides both technologically and financially.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: But even with those domestic production initiatives, the Chinese hunger for AI silicon is so deep that six months ago, Reuters said the nation's tech firms had more than two million H200 chips on order, far more than what Nvidia had on hand at the time. We'd venture that hunger has barely subsided.ZTE might not be a familiar name Stateside, but the corporation is one of China's largest telecommunication conglomerates, and among many other ventures, it sells all sorts of carrier network gear that's installed worldwide, along with corresponding client-facing equipment, including phones and IoT equipment. Like most any sizable technological venture, ZTE has joined in on the cloud computing and AI push, so it needs accelerators to make those ambitions reality. You may like Trump says China is blocking Nvidia H200 purchases despite US approval U.S. Commerce Secretary says Nvidia still hasn't sold any H200 AI GPUs to China — Chinese government is blocking imports in an attempt to push domestic semiconductor industry US closes loophole that allowed Chinese-owned subsidiaries located outside China to buy AI chips The current status of the AI chip trade situation is roughly that the U.S. allows Chinese firms to buy AI chips up to and including the Hopper family (meaning no Blackwell chips), with a 25% export tariff, though final decisions are made on a case-by-case basis. Over on Chinese shores, Beijing's authorities play their cards close to their chest and dole out approvals as they see fit, with no clear rules seemingly set. But China is, of course, a global power with trade connections to most everyone, so interested firms were able to get their hands on Blackwell chips through various creative (and potentially illicit) means.Whether this change will actually clear the way for any great volumes of H200 accelerators to make their way into ZTE's data centers remains to be seen. CNBC cites a U.S. trade official who today stated that "very few shipments against licenses for H200s and equivalents have taken place. It’s a very small quantity of chips" during a congressional hearing. If H200 shipments become material to Nvidia's bottom line, we'll almost certainly hear about it in future comments or earnings reports.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Bruno Ferreira
history.titleZh: 美国政府允许中国电信巨头中兴通讯购买英伟达H200 AI芯片——该公司与阿里巴巴、腾讯、字节跳动一同获得Hopper技术使用权
history.descriptionZh:
中美芯片战持续升级，英伟达H200芯片获准对华出口但中国本土政策成关键变量

在中美科技竞争的大背景下，两国围绕半导体技术进出口的博弈从未停歇。最新进展显示，美国已批准中国电信巨头中兴通讯（ZTE）和服务器厂商镁光（Magnifra）购买英伟达上一代H200“Hopper”系列芯片。中兴通讯由此加入了一个已包含阿里巴巴、腾讯、字节跳动、京东等约十家中国企业的“采购许可俱乐部”。此外，金山云旗下一家子公司也获得了购买与英伟达H200性能相当的AMD加速器（推测为Instinct MI300X级别）的许可。

然而，据路透社报道，中国相关部门是否会批准中兴通讯的进口申请仍是未知数。近年来，中国采取保护主义立场，大力扶持本土芯片产业，明确鼓励国内企业采购国产加速器，而非依赖外国技术。华为在本土AI芯片领域取得了显著的技术和商业突破。

即便有国产替代方案，中国科技企业对AI芯片的需求依然极其旺盛。六个月前，路透社曾披露，中国科技企业累计订购了超过200万块英伟达H200芯片，远超当时英伟达的库存量。这一需求至今未见明显消退。

中兴通讯虽在美国市场知名度有限，但作为中国最大的电信综合企业之一，其业务覆盖全球多个国家的运营商网络设备、手机及物联网终端。像所有大型科技企业一样，中兴通讯正积极布局云计算和AI领域，因此对高性能AI加速器有着迫切需求。

当前AI芯片贸易的格局大致如下：美方允许中国企业购买包括Hopper系列在内的AI芯片（但不包括最新的Blackwell架构芯片），并征收25%的出口关税，具体审批采取逐案审查制。而中方主管部门审批标准不透明，未出台明确规则。尽管如此，作为全球贸易大国，中国企业仍通过多种创新（甚至可能违规）渠道获取Blackwell芯片。

本次许可的放宽是否能让大量H200芯片真正进入中兴通讯的数据中心，仍有待观察。据CNBC援引一位美国贸易官员在国会听证会上的发言，目前“针对H200及其等效芯片许可证的实际出货量非常少，仅涉及极小规模的芯片数量”。如果未来H200出货量对英伟达财报产生实质性影响，外界很可能会在后续的业绩电话会议中听到相关披露。
